args.description: Override default container args (useful when using custom images)
args.title: args

command.description: Override default container command (useful when using custom images)
command.title: command

commonAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to all deployed objects
commonAnnotations.title: commonAnnotations

commonLabels.description: Labels to add to all deployed objects
commonLabels.title: commonLabels

configMapAnnotations.description: Additional annotations for the ConfigMap objects. Evaluated as a template
configMapAnnotations.title: configMapAnnotations

configMapLabels.description: Additional labels for the ConfigMap objects. Evaluated as a template
configMapLabels.title: configMapLabels

capabilities.description: Fine-grained Linux kernel privileges to add or drop for the container
capabilities.title: capabilities

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mounts the container root filesystem read-only to prevent in-place writes or tampering
readOnlyRootFilesystem.title: readOnlyRootFilesystem

runAsNonRoot.description: Boolean that requires the container to run as a non-root UID (prevents starting if UID 0)
runAsNonRoot.title: runAsNonRoot

runAsUser.description: UID the container processes run as (overrides container image default)
runAsUser.title: runAsUser

enabled.description: Conditional property used in parent chart
enabled.title: enabled

exampleValue.description: # It is required by CI/CD tools and processes. # @skip exampleValue #
exampleValue.title: exampleValue

extraDeploy.description: Array of extra objects to deploy with the release
extraDeploy.title: extraDeploy

extraEnvVarsCMs.description: List of ConfigMaps containing extra env vars
extraEnvVarsCMs.title: extraEnvVarsCMs

extraEnvVarsSecrets.description: List of Secrets containing extra env vars
extraEnvVarsSecrets.title: extraEnvVarsSecrets

extraOptionsSpec.description: Extra options to place under .spec (e.g. replicas, strategy, revisionHistoryLimit, etc). Evaluated as a template
extraOptionsSpec.title: extraOptionsSpec

extraOptionsTemplateSpec.description: Extra options to place under .spec.template.spec (e.g. nodeSelector, affinity, restartPolicy, etc). Evaluated as a template
extraOptionsTemplateSpec.title: extraOptionsTemplateSpec

extraVolumeMounts.description: List of volume mounts to add to the container (evaluated as template). Normally used with `extraVolumes`
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

extraVolumes.description: List of volumes to add to the deployment (evaluated as template). Requires setting `extraVolumeMounts`
extraVolumes.title: extraVolumes

imageCredentials.description: Optional credentials to log in and fetch images from a private registry. These credentials are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentials.title: imageCredentials

imageCredentialsSecrets.description: Optional list of existing Secrets containing image pull credentials to use for pulling images from private registries. These Secrets are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentialsSecrets.title: imageCredentialsSecrets

ingress.description: `ingress.*` value takes precedence when set; otherwise the global is used.

annotations.description: Annotations merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.annotations` wins on key collision. Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

defaultPathType.description: Default path type applied to ingress rules when `ingress.defaultPathType` is not set. `Prefix` works for nginx, traefik, AWS ALB, and most modern controllers.
defaultPathType.title: defaultPathType

enabled.description: Enable Ingress for this chart. OR'd with the chart's local `ingress.enabled` so setting this once at the parent enables all subchart Ingresses.
enabled.title: enabled

extraLabels.description: Extra labels merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.extraLabels` wins on key collision. Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

ingressClassName.description: Default ingress class name applied when `ingress.ingressClassName` is not set
ingressClassName.title: ingressClassName

path.description: Default path applied to ingress rules when `ingress.path` is not set. AWS ALB users should override to `/*`.
path.title: path

tls.description: TLS entries concatenated with the local `ingress.tls`. Evaluated as a template
tls.title: tls

ingress.title: ingress

mcpDomain.description: Domain where Seqera MCP listens. Evaluated as a template. Note: The OAuth redirect URL is automatically derived by appending /oauth/callback to the domain
mcpDomain.title: mcpDomain

platformServiceAddress.description: Seqera Platform Service name: can be the internal Kubernetes hostname or an external ingress hostname. Evaluated as a template. Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent `platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServiceAddress.title: platformServiceAddress

platformServicePort.description: Seqera Platform Service port. Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent `platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServicePort.title: platformServicePort

digest.description: Container image digest in the format `sha256:1234abcdef`
digest.title: digest

pullSecrets.description: List of imagePullSecrets Secrets must be created in the same namespace, for example using the .extraDeploy array Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/
pullSecrets.title: pullSecrets

annotations.description: Ingress annotations specific to your load balancer. Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

defaultBackend.description: Configure the default service for the ingress (evaluated as template) Important: make sure only one defaultBackend is defined across the k8s cluster: if the ingress doesn't reconcile successfully, 'describe ingress <name>' will report problems
defaultBackend.title: defaultBackend

defaultPathType.description: Default path type for the Ingress. When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.defaultPathType`
defaultPathType.title: defaultPathType

extraHosts.description: Additional hosts you want to include. Evaluated as a template
extraHosts.title: extraHosts

extraLabels.description: Additional labels for the ingress object. Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

ingressClassName.description: Name of the ingress class (replaces the deprecated annotation `kubernetes.io/ingress.class`). When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.ingressClassName`
ingressClassName.title: ingressClassName

path.description: Path for the main ingress rule. When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.path`
path.title: path

tls.description: TLS configuration. Evaluated as a template
tls.title: tls

enabled.description: Enable init containers that coordinate startup dependencies (for example, wait for Seqera Platform readiness before starting, etc)
enabled.title: enabled

enabled.description: Enable wait for Seqera Platform init container before starting the MCP server
enabled.title: enabled

extraEnvVars.description: Additional environment variables for the init container
extraEnvVars.title: extraEnvVars

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the init container (e.g. to mount a CA certificate)
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

digest.description: Wait for Platform init container image digest in the format `sha256:1234abcdef`
digest.title: digest

pullPolicy.description: imagePullPolicy for the wait for Platform init container
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

registry.description: Wait for Platform init container image registry
registry.title: registry

repository.description: Wait for Platform init container image repository
repository.title: repository

tag.description: Wait for Platform init container image tag
tag.title: tag

resources.description: Container requests and limits for different resources like CPU or memory
resources.title: resources

capabilities.description: Fine-grained Linux kernel privileges to add or drop for the container
capabilities.title: capabilities

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem read-only to prevent in-place writes or tampering
readOnlyRootFilesystem.title: readOnlyRootFilesystem

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root UID (prevents starting if UID 0)
runAsNonRoot.title: runAsNonRoot

runAsUser.description: UID the container processes run as (overrides container image default)
runAsUser.title: runAsUser

initContainers.description: Additional init containers for the pod. Evaluated as a template
initContainers.title: initContainers

livenessProbe.description: Configure extra options for the liveness probe

failureThreshold.description: Consecutive failures before restarting the container
failureThreshold.title: failureThreshold

path.description: HTTP GET path for liveness probe
path.title: path

port.description: HTTP GET port for liveness probe. Evaluated as a template
port.title: port

initialDelaySeconds.description: Delay before first check (normal start timing)
initialDelaySeconds.title: initialDelaySeconds

periodSeconds.description: Regular check interval during normal operation
periodSeconds.title: periodSeconds

timeoutSeconds.description: Short timeout to detect hung containers quickly
timeoutSeconds.title: timeoutSeconds

livenessProbe.title: livenessProbe

micronautEnvironments.description: List of Micronaut environments to enable. Evaluated as a template
micronautEnvironments.title: micronautEnvironments

audience.description: OAuth audience for MCP to authenticate with. This is the expected audience claim in the tokens issued by the OAuth provider. When using Seqera Platform as the OAuth provider, this should be set to "platform" to match the audience of the internal client that Platform creates for MCP. When using a custom OAuth provider, this should match the audience configured for the client that MCP uses to authenticate with that provider
audience.title: audience

issuerUrl.description: OAuth provider URL used by MCP to authenticate and obtain tokens. Defaults to the Platform API endpoint when 'oauth-platform' is configured in `.micronautEnvironments` (default behavior). Must be set explicitly when 'oauth' is configured in `.micronautEnvironments`.
issuerUrl.title: issuerUrl

jwtSeedSecretKey.description: Key in the existing Secret containing the JWT seed
jwtSeedSecretKey.title: jwtSeedSecretKey

jwtSeedSecretName.description: Name of an existing Secret containing the JWT seed, as an alternative to the string field. Note: the Secret must already exist in the same namespace at the time of deployment
jwtSeedSecretName.title: jwtSeedSecretName

jwtSeedString.description: JWT seed, defined as string, used to sign authentication tokens. Define the value as a String or a Secret, not both at the same time. If neither is defined, Helm generates a random 35-character string. WARNING: Always explicitly set this value or use an existing secret when using Kustomize. Auto-generated random values are incompatible with Kustomize. When upgrading releases via Kustomize, Helm cannot query the cluster to check if a secret already exists, causing it to regenerate a new random value on each upgrade, which will break authentication
jwtSeedString.title: jwtSeedString

existingSecretKey.description: Key in the existing Secret containing the OIDC client registration token.
existingSecretKey.title: existingSecretKey

existingSecretName.description: Name of an existing Secret containing the OIDC client registration token, as an alternative to the string field. Note: the Secret must already exist in the same namespace at the time of deployment.
existingSecretName.title: existingSecretName

tokenString.description: OIDC client registration token as a string. Used to dynamically register an OAuth client with Seqera Platform's OIDC provider. If neither this nor existingSecretName is set, a random value is generated. When deployed via the platform parent chart, this is automatically defined with the value of the OIDC client registration token from the platform backend secret, so it should not be set in that case. When deploying independently of the platform parent chart, this must be set to the same value defined in the platform backend secret. WARNING: Always explicitly set this value or use an existing secret when using Kustomize. Auto-generated random values are incompatible with Kustomize. When upgrading releases via Kustomize, Helm cannot query the cluster to check if a secret already exists, causing it to regenerate a new random value on each upgrade, which will break authentication
tokenString.title: tokenString

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations for the pod. Evaluated as a template
podAnnotations.title: podAnnotations

podLabels.description: Additional labels for the pod. Evaluated as a template
podLabels.title: podLabels

fsGroup.description: Sets the GID that Kubernetes will apply to mounted volumes and created files so processes in the pod can share group-owned access
fsGroup.title: fsGroup

failureThreshold.title: failureThreshold

path.description: HTTP GET path for readiness probe
path.title: path

port.description: HTTP GET port for readiness probe. Evaluated as a template
port.title: port

initialDelaySeconds.description: Delay before first check (normal start timing)
initialDelaySeconds.title: initialDelaySeconds

periodSeconds.description: Regular check interval during normal operation
periodSeconds.title: periodSeconds

successThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successes required to mark the container Ready after failures
successThreshold.title: successThreshold

timeoutSeconds.description: Short timeout to detect unresponsive containers for readiness
timeoutSeconds.title: timeoutSeconds

readinessProbe.title: readinessProbe

registryApiEndpoint.description: API endpoint of Seqera Nextflow Registry.
registryApiEndpoint.title: registryApiEndpoint

resources.description: Container requests and limits for different resources like CPU or memory
resources.title: resources

secretAnnotations.description: Additional annotations for the Secret objects. Evaluated as a template
secretAnnotations.title: secretAnnotations

secretLabels.description: Additional labels for the Secret objects. Evaluated as a template
secretLabels.title: secretLabels

extraOptions.description: Extra Service options to place under .spec (for example, clusterIP, loadBalancerIP, externalTrafficPolicy, externalIPs). Evaluated as a template
extraOptions.title: extraOptions

extraServices.description: Other services that should live in the Service object https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#defining-a-service
extraServices.title: extraServices

nodePort.description: Service node port, only used when service.type is Nodeport or LoadBalancer Choose port between 30000-32767, unless the cluster was configured differently than default
nodePort.title: nodePort

targetPort.description: Port on the pod/container that the Service forwards traffic to (can be a number or named port, distinct from the Service's external port)
targetPort.title: targetPort

type.description: Service type. Note: ingresses using AWS ALB require the service to be NodePort
type.title: type

imagePullSecretNames.description: Names of Secrets containing credentials to pull images from registries
imagePullSecretNames.title: imagePullSecretNames

startupProbe.description: Configure extra options for the startup probe

failureThreshold.description: Consecutive failures during startup before killing the container (instead of immediate restarts)
failureThreshold.title: failureThreshold

path.description: HTTP GET path for startup probe
path.title: path

port.description: HTTP GET port for startup probe. Evaluated as a template
port.title: port

initialDelaySeconds.description: Longer initial wait to accommodate slow-starting apps
initialDelaySeconds.title: initialDelaySeconds

periodSeconds.description: Often set longer to avoid frequent checks while starting
periodSeconds.title: periodSeconds

successThreshold.description: Number of consecutive successes required to consider startup complete and enable liveness/readiness
successThreshold.title: successThreshold

timeoutSeconds.description: Can be longer to allow slow initialization responses
timeoutSeconds.title: timeoutSeconds

startupProbe.title: startupProbe
